name: Vidalia Onion Flower
recipeInstructions:
1. With a sharp knife, carefully peel the onion and trim the root end, leaving it intact.
2. Standing the onion on its root end, cut parallel vertical slices at 1/4-inch intervals into, but not through, the onion, stopping about 1/2 inch above the root end.
3. Rotate the onion 90 degrees; cut parallel vertical slices in the same manner to form a crosshatch pattern, keeping the onion intact.
4. Coat a microwave-safe pie plate with vegetable spray.
5. Place the onion, root end down, in the center of the pie plate.
6. Drizzle with margarine and season with salt and pepper to taste.
7. Cover with waxed paper and microwave on High (100% power) for 3 minutes.
8. Baste with cooking liquid and rotate the dish.
9. Cover and microwave on High for 3 to 4 minutes longer, or until the onion spreads open into a "flower."
10. Sprinkle the flower with pecans; microwave on High for 1 to 2 minutes longer, or until the flower appears translucent.
11. With a wide spatula, remove the onion flower from the pie plate to a serving plate.
Per serving: About 65 calories, 1 gram protein, 12 grams carbohydrates, 2 grams fat, 28% of calories from fat, 0 milligrams cholesterol, 31 milligrams sodium.
